content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reject the scope-label stripping amendment as intentional, tested behavior with negligible blast radius. Accept both the deterministic-rendering and acceptance-ownership amendments as genuine correctness defects.

## Votes

- `fr-7-f80c2e20`: Reject
  The label-stripping behavior in `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary` is intentional normalization: the function's purpose is to extract a concise one-line summary from potentially labeled markdown, and three explicit tests validate the label-stripping path (inline body extraction and label-only line skipping). The edge case where a scope label word (e.g., "Summary") is also part of the feature name is extremely narrow, and even when triggered the result is still a meaningful summary ("Build a summary export pipeline" rather than the full labeled line). Additionally, since the function returns on the first substantive line, the ambiguity only matters when the very first content line starts with a recognized label — which is the exact scenario the normalization is designed for. Changing the rule would regress the intentional behavior without commensurate benefit.
- `fr-7-464e51de`: Accept
  Converting `AppError::Io` from `load_bead_summaries` into an empty `BTreeMap` (lines 296-300) makes prompt rendering depend on the transient availability of `br list --json` rather than solely on project state. When summaries are absent, `prompt_bead_status(summary, None)` returns "unknown" (line 1159) and titles fall back to proposal-level data or bead IDs (line 1362), producing different prompt.md content and hashes for identical milestone/bead states. This is a real determinism defect: prompt hashes should be a pure function of project data, not of whether an external tool happens to be reachable. The fix should either propagate IO errors as hard failures or source the needed status/title fields from data already available in the milestone bundle.
- `fr-7-e92e36d4`: Accept
  `build_planned_elsewhere_context` discovers related beads through three channels: direct dependents/dependencies, and +/-1 same-workstream neighbors. It never consults `acceptance_map[].covered_by`, which is the authoritative record of which beads own work toward shared milestone acceptance criteria. This means a bead in a different workstream that shares acceptance criterion ownership but has no direct dependency edge will be invisible in the planned-elsewhere section. The data is already present in the bundle (`AcceptanceCriterion.covered_by: Vec<String>` at bundle.rs:713) and would directly close the gap — beads covering the same acceptance criterion as the current bead should appear as planned-elsewhere context to prevent reviewers from demanding scope expansion into work that is already assigned elsewhere.
